notes:
WotC Neo-era set (released 2001-09-21, pre-2002 -> NO reverse-holo slot per the era rule). Booster structure confirmed across sources: 11 physical cards/pack modeled as the engine's WotC baseline 7 common + 3 uncommon + 1 rare-or-better (2 basic Energy carry no EV, not modeled). Booster box = 36 packs; NO ETB existed in this era -> etb=null.

HOLO RATE: Holographic Rares ~1 in 3 packs across the Neo series (Flipside Gaming: "Holo (approximately 1:3 packs)"; DA Card World Neo Revelation booster-box listing corroborates the ~1-in-3 foil chance). Single rare-or-better slot resolves to rare-holo = 1/3 = 0.333, rare = 2/3 = 0.667. Sums to exactly 1.0.

BUCKET MAPPING: available buckets {rare-holo:14, rare:15, uncommon:17, common:20}. Bulbapedia rarity breakdown (re-fetched): 14 Holographic Rare, 10 Rare, 14 Uncommon, 26 Common, 2 Shining Holo (#65-66). rare-holo:14 is an EXACT match to Bulbapedia's 14 Holographic Rares. Neo Revelation introduced SHINING Pokemon (Shining Gyarados/Magikarp) = the era's secret rares, pulled ~1:18 packs (Flipside). This set's buckets include NO secret-rare key, so per the integrity rule (keys must be a subset of available buckets) the Shining cards are NOT given a separate bucket — they fall inside rare-holo and their ~1:18 incidence is a subset of the ~1:3 holo slot. Slot economy depends only on the sourced holo-vs-rare split.

CONFIDENCE 'low': no per-set published pull-rate study for this vintage set; the 1:3 holo rate is an era-average from community/retailer sources, consistent with the WotC baseline. Vintage sealed boxes carry a large collector premium, so EV/booster will sit far below box price (expected; rates not inflated). File written to /Users/alexandreott/Desktop/POKÉ EV/data/pull-rates/neo-revelation.json and passes `npx tsx scripts/validate-data.ts` ("All datasets valid").
